title: Trump Says Iran Proposal Isn’t Enough to Stop Attacks on Bridges and Power Plants
summary: President Trump has told Iran it must open the Strait of Hormuz by 8 p.m. Tuesday or face the consequences, although he has delayed previous deadlines.

title: Hegseth Likens Easter Rescue of U.S. Airman to Resurrection of Jesus Christ
summary: President Trump also asserted that God supports the American war against Iran “because God is good, and God wants to see people taken care of.”

title: Trump Threatens Jail if Journalists Protect Certain Iran Sources
summary: The president indicated he would ask an unnamed media outlet to reveal the sources behind its coverage of Iran’s successful strike on a U.S. fighter jet, and of its crew.

title: Trump Threatens ‘Complete Demolition’ by Midnight, and Dark Money Flows Into Midterms
summary: Plus, a look behind the moon.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Head Home After Historic Journey Around the Moon
summary: The NASA lunar flyby took the four crew members farther from Earth than any humans. They witnessed a solar eclipse and received praise in a call from President Trump.

title: Vance Visits Hungary to Boost Orban Before Election
summary: The trip by the American vice president, JD Vance, makes clear that Russia is not the only country invested in a victory for Hungary’s leader, Viktor Orban.

title: Why Hungary’s Election Could Swing on Roma Votes
summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s policies affecting the Roma minority have put those voters in play in upcoming parliamentary elections. In a tight race, they could make the difference.

title: Texas Considers Required Reading List for Schools, Which Includes the Bible
summary: Education officials are planning an overhaul to English and social studies in the nation’s largest Republican led state.

title: Idaho Cut Services for People With Schizophrenia. Then the Deaths Began.
summary: Eliminating outreach to people with severe mental illness set off such a cascade of bad outcomes that Idaho has scrambled to reverse the cuts.

title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square Bids to Buy Universal Music Group
summary: The complex transaction pitched by the billionaire hedge fund manager would need to pass muster with the music label’s shareholders.

title: ‘No Labels’ Arizona Wants to Rebrand as the Independent Party
summary: Both the Democratic and Republican parties in Arizona have been locked in a legal battle with a chapter of the group “No Labels” as it tries to rechristen itself.

title: Without Elon Musk, Wisconsin’s Supreme Court Justice Election Goes Quiet
summary: Elections for the Wisconsin Supreme Court have previously brought record-breaking spending and national attention. Tuesday’s race has been a more muted affair.

title: What to Watch in the Election to Succeed Marjorie Taylor Greene in Georgia
summary: Clay Fuller, a Republican allied with President Trump, will face Shawn Harris, a Democrat, in the election to fill the remainder of Ms. Greene’s term after her resignation from Congress.

title: Texas Congressman Accused of Pursuing a Second Subordinate With Lewd Texts
summary: A news report linked Representative Tony Gonzales of Texas, who has admitted to an affair with an aide, to another series of sexual texts with a different aide, raising a dilemma for the House G.O.P.

title: How Accurate Are Google’s A.I. Overviews?
summary: The company’s A.I.-generated answers look authoritative, but they draw on an array of sources, from trustworthy sites to Facebook posts.

title: Why Am I Watching People Get Their Medical Results?
summary: What was once discussed with a doctor is now frequently encountered first as decontextualized data on a screen.

title: Europe’s Museums Confront the (Literal) Skeletons in Their Closets
summary: Institutions are grappling with the human remains in their collections that were used to justify debunked theories about race.

title: In Paris’s Catacombs, Can a Restoration Breathe New Life Into City’s Dead?
summary: For centuries, the bones of some six million people were buried in the catacombs beneath the city. Curators are trying to preserve and modernize the tunnels while maintaining the spooky ambience.

title: Pakistan Says It Hit a Military Target. Investigations Suggest It Was a Rehab Center.
summary: After the deadliest attack in Afghanistan since the Taliban took over, families searched among photos and remains for signs of their relatives.

title: How to Build a Rest Stop for Delivery Workers in a Hurry
summary: A shelter for delivery workers was proposed years ago. Then Mayor Zohran Mamdani decided that it needed to be finished within his first 100 days in office.

title: What’s Your Wedding Style? Take Our Quiz to Find Out.
summary: From a secret elopement in the mountains to a weeklong blowout beach bash, every couple has a different idea on how they want to say “I do.” This quiz is designed to help you decide which wedding style feels most like you.

title: How Much Humiliation Can JD Vance Take?
summary: The vice president gets saddled with the Iran war and eroding popularity.

title: He Was an Insurance Executive. She’s a Doctor. They’re Divided.
summary: We put a doctor and a former insurance executive in a room.

title: The Far Side
summary: The Artemis II crew has gone farther from Earth than anyone has ever been.

title: Stephen Colbert Mocks Trump’s Expletive-Filled Post Threatening Iran
summary: Late night hosts mocked the president’s expletive-filled ultimatum to Iran, posted on Easter Sunday morning.

title: Gunman Killed in Shootout Near Israeli Consulate in Istanbul, Officials Say
summary: Two other armed men and two police officers were wounded in the clash, the provincial governor said.

title: Offset, Former Migos Rapper, Is Shot Outside Florida Casino
summary: The former member of the chart-topping Atlanta trio was in stable condition after being shot in Hollywood, Fla., his representative said. The police said they had detained two people.

title: Presto! In Chicago, a High-End Magic Palace Appears
summary: The entrepreneur Glen Tullman is betting people want to dress up and watch magicians in a luxury setting. Either it will work or $50 million will go poof.

title: Justice Dept. Resolves Broadway Touring Company Investigation
summary: Broadway Across America will not be prosecuted after acknowledging it signed a noncompete agreement with another presenter, the agency said.
